refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥ 0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as (total scheduled operating time − unplanned downtime) / total scheduled operating time, accounting for all failure modes across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that directly impacts customer value and mission success. Refinement clarifies the measurement methodology, evaluation period, and scope of downtime contributors. High safety criticality requires explicit inclusion of safety-related failures in availability accounting. The metric must be measurable and traceable to subsystem reliability targets.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous telemetry logging of operational state transitions (ready, operating, failed, maintenance); automated availability calculation dashboard; post-evaluation period audit against agreed baseline; root cause analysis of all downtime events exceeding threshold.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: Vehicle subsystem shall contribute ≤ 3% unplanned downtime annually
FR-NGHE-004.2: Teleoperation subsystem shall maintain ≥ 99.5% network link availability during scheduled operations
FR-NGHE-004.3: Safety Services subsystem shall detect and report critical failures within 100 ms to prevent unsafe operation
FR-NGHE-004.4: Maintenance procedures shall be executable within 4-hour windows to minimize scheduled downtime impact

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundant subsystem architectures (dual vehicle control paths, backup teleoperation network, safety watchdog circuits) with automatic failover and health monitoring
DP-B: Establish predictive maintenance program using telemetry analytics to schedule maintenance during planned downtime windows, reducing unplanned failures
DP-C: Design modular component architecture enabling rapid hot-swap replacement of failed modules without full system shutdown
DP-D: Implement comprehensive fault detection, isolation, and recovery (FDIR) logic across all subsystems with automated state transitions
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A + DP-B + DP-D (integrated approach): Implement redundant subsystem architectures with automatic failover, establish predictive maintenance analytics to shift failures to planned windows, and deploy comprehensive FDIR logic with automated recovery. This combination directly addresses both random failures (redundancy) and wear-out failures (predictive maintenance) while maintaining safety criticality through deterministic fault detection.

potential_sources: Redundancy adds complexity to teleoperation network management (DP-A couples with teleoperation subsystem design)
Predictive maintenance requires continuous telemetry ingestion (DP-B couples with Vehicle and Safety Services telemetry interfaces)
FDIR logic must coordinate across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services (DP-D creates cross-subsystem dependencies)
coupling_check.notes: Couplings are manageable through well-defined interface contracts (Telemetry, SafetyAlert). The selected DP avoids the independence axiom violation by ensuring each subsystem owns its redundancy and FDIR logic; coordination occurs only at the NGHE Solution level through standardized interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Telemetry bandwidth is sufficient to support continuous health monitoring from all redundant paths without network saturation.
Assumption: Predictive maintenance models can be trained on historical failure data; if data is sparse, model accuracy may be insufficient.
Risk: Redundancy increases cost and weight; customer may reject if availability target can be met with simpler design.
Risk: FDIR logic complexity increases verification burden; safety-critical FDIR paths require formal methods or extensive testing.
Risk: Automatic failover may mask underlying failures, delaying root cause identification and repair.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A to specify redundancy scope (e.g., 'dual independent control channels for critical safety functions; single-channel with watchdog for non-critical functions'). Refine DP-B to define predictive maintenance trigger thresholds and confidence levels. Refine DP-D to specify FDIR response times and safe states for each failure mode.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level availability orchestration module responsible for: (1) aggregating health status from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems via Telemetry interface; (2) executing availability monitoring and KPI calculation logic; (3) coordinating failover decisions and maintenance scheduling; (4) exposing availability metrics to Site Manager and Project Manager stakeholders.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives continuous health status, operational state, and failure events from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Used to calculate real-time availability metrics and trigger predictive maintenance analytics.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives critical safety failure notifications from Safety Services subsystem. Triggers immediate failover or safe shutdown to prevent unsafe operation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends maintenance scheduling directives and operational mode changes (e.g., 'enter maintenance window', 'activate redundant path') to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for telemetry ingestion and tasking dispatch; availability of Network Link directly impacts NGHE Solution's ability to monitor and coordinate subsystems.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highest containment: availability orchestration is a system-level concern that logically belongs at the NGHE Solution boundary. Telemetry aggregation, KPI calculation, and failover coordination are naturally centralized here. Minimal diffusion: only interfaces with subsystems via standard contracts.

Low containment: Vehicle is a subsystem, not the system orchestrator. Assigning availability KPI logic to Vehicle would scatter coordination logic across subsystems and couple Vehicle design to teleoperation and safety concerns. High diffusion risk.

Low containment: Teleoperation is a subsystem focused on remote operation. Assigning system-level availability orchestration here would create inappropriate coupling and make Vehicle and Safety Services dependent on Teleoperation for availability decisions. High diffusion risk.

Low containment: Safety Services is responsible for safety-critical fault detection and alerting, not system-level availability orchestration. Mixing availability KPI logic with safety logic violates separation of concerns and complicates safety certification. High diffusion risk.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: System Engineer (with support from Maintenance/Quality for predictive maintenance analytics and Safety Manager for FDIR safety validation)
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Availability KPI orchestration is a system-level architectural concern that must be owned at the NGHE Solution boundary to avoid diffusion across subsystems and maintain clear separation of concerns. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own their respective subsystem reliability; NGHE Solution owns the aggregation, coordination, and KPI calculation.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution module is not properly isolated, availability logic may leak into subsystem designs, creating circular dependencies.
Risk: Telemetry interface may become a bottleneck if availability monitoring requires high-frequency polling; latency in telemetry ingestion could delay failover decisions.
Risk: Predictive maintenance analytics may require machine learning models that are difficult to certify for safety-critical use; unclear ownership of model validation.
Risk: Network Link availability directly impacts NGHE Solution's ability to monitor subsystems; single point of failure if not properly redundant.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define NGHE Solution module as a 'system orchestration layer' with clear ownership of availability KPI logic, failover coordination, and predictive maintenance scheduling. Establish interface contracts (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking) with defined latency and reliability requirements. Clarify that Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own subsystem-level reliability; NGHE Solution owns system-level availability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (redundancy + predictive maintenance + FDIR) directly addresses FR requirement for ≥ 0.97 availability. DP is traceable to FR and measurable. Coupling analysis confirms that redundancy and FDIR logic can be distributed across subsystems with coordination at NGHE Solution level, avoiding independence axiom violation. Assumptions and risks are documented and manageable.

diffusion_risks: Availability orchestration logic must remain centralized in NGHE Solution; risk of diffusion if subsystems attempt to implement their own availability KPI calculations.
Predictive maintenance analytics may require shared data models across subsystems; risk of tight coupling if not properly abstracted through Telemetry interface.
FDIR logic coordination across subsystems requires clear ownership boundaries; risk of diffusion if failover decisions are made at multiple levels.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution module provides appropriate containment for system-level availability orchestration. Subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) own their respective reliability mechanisms; NGHE Solution owns aggregation and coordination. Interface contracts (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link) provide clear boundaries and minimize diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Availability KPI is measurable via continuous telemetry logging and automated dashboard. Verification plan includes: (1) subsystem-level MTBF/MTTR testing, (2) system-level availability simulation, (3) field trial measurement over agreed evaluation period, (4) root cause analysis of downtime events.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-004 is allocated to NGHE Solution (system-level KPI orchestration). Sub-FRs are allocated to Vehicle (FR-004.1), Teleoperation (FR-004.2), Safety Services (FR-004.3), and Maintenance (FR-004.4). Clear traceability from system FR to subsystem FRs and implementation modules.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP to specify redundancy scope and FDIR response time requirements (e.g., 'failover within 100 ms for safety-critical functions').
Define predictive maintenance trigger thresholds and confidence levels to avoid false positives that increase scheduled downtime.
Establish interface contracts for Telemetry (frequency, latency, reliability) and SafetyAlert (latency, priority) to support availability orchestration.
Clarify ownership of FDIR logic validation and safety certification; coordinate with Safety Manager.
Document assumptions about customer availability target, evaluation period, and downtime accounting methodology.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that requires decomposition into subsystem-level reliability requirements (FR-004.1 through FR-004.4) to enable detailed design and verification. The selected DP (redundancy + predictive maintenance + FDIR) is sound and traceable to FR, but implementation requires further refinement at the subsystem level. NGHE Solution module provides appropriate containment for system-level orchestration, but subsystem modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) must be decomposed to allocate reliability targets and design redundancy/FDIR mechanisms.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: Vehicle subsystem shall contribute ≤ 3% unplanned downtime annually (allocated to Vehicle module)
FR-NGHE-004.2: Teleoperation subsystem shall maintain ≥ 99.5% network link availability during scheduled operations (allocated to Teleoperation module)
FR-NGHE-004.3: Safety Services subsystem shall detect and report critical failures within 100 ms to prevent unsafe operation (allocated to Safety Services module)
FR-NGHE-004.4: Maintenance procedures shall be executable within 4-hour windows to minimize scheduled downtime impact (allocated to Maintenance/Quality)

assumptions: Customer-defined availability target is ≥ 0.97 (97%) over agreed evaluation period (e.g., 12 months).
Unplanned downtime includes all failures in Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems; scheduled maintenance is excluded.
Telemetry bandwidth and Network Link reliability are sufficient to support continuous health monitoring without saturation.
Predictive maintenance models can be trained on historical failure data; model accuracy is ≥ 80% for failure prediction.
Redundancy and FDIR logic can be implemented within cost and weight constraints acceptable to customer.
Safety-critical FDIR paths can be certified through formal methods or extensive testing within project schedule.
open_risks: Redundancy cost and weight may exceed customer budget; availability target may need to be relaxed or alternative DP selected.
Predictive maintenance model accuracy may be insufficient if historical failure data is sparse; risk of false positives increasing scheduled downtime.
FDIR logic complexity increases verification burden; safety certification may require external review and extend schedule.
Automatic failover may mask underlying failures, delaying root cause identification and repair; risk of repeated failures.
Network Link availability directly impacts system availability; single point of failure if not properly redundant.
Subsystem-level reliability targets (FR-004.1 through FR-004.3) may be infeasible with current technology; risk of unachievable availability KPI.
evidence_needed: Historical failure data from similar equipment to validate predictive maintenance model accuracy and subsystem MTBF/MTTR estimates.
Cost and weight analysis of redundancy options to confirm feasibility within customer constraints.
Safety analysis (FMEA, FTA) to identify critical failure modes and FDIR response requirements.
Network architecture analysis to confirm Network Link redundancy and availability targets.
Verification plan detailing subsystem-level testing, system-level simulation, and field trial measurement methodology.
Customer acceptance criteria and evaluation period definition to ensure alignment on availability measurement.
